text: This dimly lit room hums quietly with the presence of technology. The glow of a monitor provides minimal illumination, casting uncertain shadows. Surrounded by machines, cables, and scientific detritus, I feel an odd calmness tinged with isolation. It is as if I am nestled in a cocoon of thought—the world outside distant, the closest company the faint whir of servers. Here, introspection becomes almost inescapable; technology both connects and separates me from the wider world. I wonder: is this solitude or a form of communion?
